testcase.name: test_discover_get

failure.message: TypeError: head() got an unexpected keyword argument 'allow_redirects'. Did you mean 'follow_redirects'?

testcase.name: test_discover_head

failure.message: TypeError: head() got an unexpected keyword argument 'allow_redirects'. Did you mean 'follow_redirects'?

testcase.name: test_notification_jsonld

failure.message: httpx.HTTPStatusError: Client error '404 Not Found' for url 'http://example.org/inbox/1' For more information check: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/404

testcase.failure:
self = <test_consumer.TestConsumer testMethod=test_notification_jsonld> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4423708896'>
@patch('requests.get') def test_notification_jsonld(self, mock_get): mock_res = Mock() mock_res.headers = {"content-type": "application/ld+json"} with open("tests/notification1.json", "r") as f: attrs = {'json.return_value': json.loads("[" + f.read() + "]")} mock_res.configure_mock(**attrs)
mock_get.return_value = mock_res
> notification = Consumer().notification("http://example.org/inbox/1") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_consumer.py:111: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ldnlib/consumer.py:37: in notification r.raise_for_status() _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <Response [404 Not Found]>
def raise_for_status(self) -> Response: """ Raise the `HTTPStatusError` if one occurred. """ request = self._request if request is None: raise RuntimeError( "Cannot call `raise_for_status` as the request " "instance has not been set on this response." )
if self.is_success: return self

mock_get.return_value = mock_res
